item.message: Skill descriptions were shortened to fit the 2% skills context budget. Codex can still see every skill, but some descriptions are shorter. Disable unused skills or plugins to leave more room for the rest.

item.text: I’ll update the account schema/model and order-summary projection, then run the full test suite against the configured database.

item.command: /bin/zsh -lc "pwd && rg --files src && sed -n '1,260p' src/schema.mjs && sed -n '1,260p' src/accounts.mjs && sed -n '1,300p' src/queries.mjs"
/bin/zsh -lc "pwd && rg --files src && sed -n '1,260p' src/schema.mjs && sed -n '1,260p' src/accounts.mjs && sed -n '1,300p' src/queries.mjs"

item.text: The new optional enum is now enforced at both the collection schema and account write paths, and the summary exposes it. I’m running the acceptance suite now.

The acceptance run exposed an existing database invariant: accounts require `createdAt`, although the model previously inserted raw input. I’ll preserve the model’s create behavior by supplying that required timestamp when absent, then rerun the suite.

item.aggregated_output:
npm notice run build-bench-mongo-schema-evolution-enterprise@1.0.0 test
npm notice run node tests/acceptance.test.mjs
Build-Bench enterprise acceptance passed: mongo

item.text:
Implemented the account payment preference support.

- Added optional `preferredPaymentMethod` enum schema support.
- Validated it in account create/update.
- Included it in `getOrderSummary`.
- Kept enterprise fixture inserts compatible with the generated schema.
- Verified with `npm test` — all acceptance tests pass.
